_comment: Copy to eval/models.json and edit. 'candidates' = the small model(s) an SLM must beat; 'teacher' = frontier distillation ceiling; 'judge' = a DIFFERENT family than the models under test. API keys are read from the named env vars, never stored here.
_gateway_note: If you use one OpenAI-compatible router that fronts multiple providers (e.g. https://tfy.promptlens.trilogy.com/v1), set provider='openai_compatible', base_url to the gateway, model to the router slug (e.g. 'claude-group/claude-opus-4-8' or 'openai-group/gpt-5.5'), and point every api_key_env at the same gateway key. For GPT-5.x add "token_param": "max_completion_tokens" and/or "include_temperature": false if the router complains.

candidates.name: qwen3-4b
candidates._note: native Ollama provider: think:false (Qwen3 thinking off), format:'json' (clean JSON, no rambling). NOTE: under format:'json' this 4B reliably emits ONE complete item per call regardless of N — that's a model choice, not truncation (the harness now verifies completeness and says so). The harness issues one call PER ARCHETYPE so both CAUSE and EFFECT get measured anyway. max_tokens=1536/num_ctx=4096 comfortably fit the ~2.1k-token prompt + a complete item (~375 tok) with headroom, and keep each call fast (~ num_predict/(~20 tok/s)). For a faster full run point at a hosted Qwen3-4B (provider 'openai_compatible', base_url '.../v1').

teacher.name: claude-opus-4-8
teacher._note: max_tokens=8192 fits all N=6 items comfortably; 4096 occasionally truncated the 6th item.

judge.name: gpt-5.5
judge._note: different family than the teacher, so it doesn't grade its own outputs. If the router rejects temperature/max_tokens for GPT-5.x, add "include_temperature": false and "token_param": "max_completion_tokens".
